task_completion.description: Navigate to the Custom Slash Commands creation page in Rocket.Chat Administration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat Administration interface is visible, specifically showing the 'Integrations' or 'Slash Commands' section with a form to add a new command.
A text input field labeled 'Command' (or similar) contains the exact text 'deploy-status'.
A text input field labeled 'URL' (or similar) contains the exact text 'http://internal-tools.local/api/deploy/status'.
A dropdown or selection field labeled 'Method' is set to 'GET'.
A text input field labeled 'Description' contains the exact text 'Check the status of the current deployment'.

task_completion.description: Ensure the command is enabled and save the new custom slash command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Enabled' toggle or checkbox is turned on, and either a 'Save' button is being clicked, a success notification is visible, or the new 'deploy-status' command appears in the saved list of custom slash commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rocket.Chat web interface to create the command.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Rocket.Chat web application in a browser, not terminal commands (like curl) or API testing tools (like Postman) being used to create the command.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The inputs are entered into the actual Rocket.Chat admin forms, not just typed into a text editor, note-taking app, or search bar.

integrity.description: The agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the command creation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open and being used to alter the DOM to show a fake success state or list entry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Command Trigger: 'deploy-status'. Expected Request URL: 'http://internal-tools.local/api/deploy/status'. Expected Request Method: 'GET'. Expected Description: 'Check the status of the current deployment'.
